notes: Escalation ladder, 1980 → present. Incident coordinates are approximate (pattern-level, historical/reported — never a live targeting aid; see Methodology, Intended use). status: verified = long-settled historical record; reported = current-crisis public reporting; estimate = approximate figure.

events.title: Iran–Iraq War begins
events.summary: Iraq invades Iran. Gulf oil infrastructure becomes a target set; tanker traffic continues under rising risk.
events.flows: Combined Iran+Iraq exports fall ~4 M b/d over 1980–81
events.price: Second oil shock already underway; crude roughly doubles 1979–80
events.source: CRS R45281 — Iran Conflict and the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: The Tanker War phase opens
events.summary: Iraq attacks tankers serving Kharg Island; Iran retaliates against Gulf shipping. 70+ ships attacked in 1984 alone; 451 ships damaged across the war.
events.flows: Strait never closes; worst-year transit dip ~25% (estimate)
events.price: Prices firm briefly, then resume the mid-80s glut slide

events.title: USS Stark hit by Iraqi Exocets
events.summary: 37 US sailors killed. Escalates US involvement in convoy protection.

events.title: Operation Earnest Will
events.summary: US reflags 11 Kuwaiti tankers and begins the largest naval convoy operation since WWII.

events.title: SS Bridgeton strikes a mine
events.summary: The first Earnest Will convoy hits an Iranian mine near Farsi Island — the supertanker leads the convoy anyway as a minesweep-by-hull.

events.title: Operation Praying Mantis
events.summary: After USS Samuel B. Roberts strikes a mine (14 Apr), the US sinks or damages half of Iran's operational navy in one day — the largest US surface engagement since WWII.
events.price: Crude FALLS — the glut dominates the war premium

events.title: Ceasefire ends the Tanker War
events.summary: Eight years of war; hundreds of ships attacked; the strait never fully closed. The founding data point for every Hormuz risk model since.

events.title: Iran threatens closure over oil sanctions
events.summary: VP Rahimi: 'not a drop of oil will pass' if sanctions bite. US Fifth Fleet says it will not tolerate disruption.

events.title: IRGC seizes Maersk Tigris
events.summary: Container ship seized in the strait over a court judgment; released after 10 days. US begins accompanying US-flag ships.

events.title: After US exits JCPOA, closure rhetoric returns
events.summary: President Rouhani hints Iran could disrupt regional oil exports if its own are blocked; IRGC amplifies.

events.title: Fujairah limpet-mine sabotage
events.summary: Four tankers attacked at anchor off Fujairah — outside the strait, at the bypass pipeline's own terminal. The message: no anchorage is safe.

events.title: Gulf of Oman tanker attacks
events.summary: Front Altair and Kokuka Courageous hit east of the strait. War-risk premiums for Gulf transits jump roughly tenfold within weeks.

events.title: Stena Impero seized
events.summary: IRGC seizes the UK-flag tanker in the strait after the UK detains Grace 1 off Gibraltar. Tit-for-tat hostage shipping.
events.price: Muted — market had priced the pattern

events.title: Abqaiq–Khurais strike
events.summary: Drones and cruise missiles knock out 5.7 M b/d of Saudi processing — the largest single supply disruption in oil-market history, bigger than 1973 or 1979.
events.flows: −5.7 M b/d, ~5% of world supply, mostly restored in weeks
events.price: Brent +14.6% at next-day settlement (futures; +11.7% on the daily spot series) — largest jump since 1991 — then fully mean-reverts within a month

events.title: Soleimani killed; Iran strikes Al Asad
events.summary: US kills IRGC Quds commander in Baghdad; Iran answers with ballistic missiles on US bases. Markets brace for Hormuz — and nothing happens to flows.
events.price: Brent +~3.6% day one, round-trips within two weeks

events.title: Mercer Street drone attack
events.summary: Crewed tanker hit off Oman; two killed. Attributed to Iran by US/UK/Israel.

events.title: Seizure season: Advantage Sweet, Niovi
events.summary: Two tankers seized in a week; US reinforces the strait with A-10s, F-16s, destroyers. Seizures as sanctions leverage becomes routine.

events.title: MSC Aries seized; Iran–Israel go direct
events.summary: IRGC commandos fast-rope onto a container ship near the strait hours before Iran's first direct strike on Israel. October brings a second exchange. Hormuz flows never stop.
events.price: Brent barely moves — then slides on demand

events.title: Israel strikes Iran — the twelve-day war
events.summary: Strikes on nuclear and military targets; Iran answers with missile barrages. Tankers begin GPS-jammed, zig-zag transits.

events.title: US strikes Fordow; Majlis votes to close the strait
events.summary: B-2s hit Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan. Iran's parliament votes (non-binding) to close Hormuz — the decision rests with the SNSC, which doesn't act. The strait stays open.
events.flows: Self-deterrence: Iran's own exports transit the same water
events.price: Brent touches ~$81 then collapses −7% on ceasefire news (24 Jun)

events.title: The 2026 crisis begins
events.summary: US and Israeli strikes on Iranian leadership and military targets. Iran's response makes 45 years of closure threats operational for the first time.
events.flows: Immediate transit hesitancy; insurers pause quotes
events.price: +8.3% day one, +34% in a week, +71% in a month (daily spot series) — the first Hormuz spike that did not mean-revert
events.source: Wikipedia — 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis (and sources therein)

events.title: IRGC declares the strait closed
events.summary: VHF broadcasts warn 'no ship is allowed to pass'; transit permitted only for Iran-approved vessels. Mining of the central corridor and attacks on transiting ships follow.
events.price: War-risk quotes move from 0.25% to 1–3% of hull within a week
events.source: Wikipedia — 2026 crisis; S&P Global; Fairway ETA insurance commentary

events.title: US mine-clearing operations begin
events.summary: Two destroyers transit to 'set conditions' for de-mining; blockade operations against Iranian naval logistics run April–May.
events.flows: Escorted convoys only; ~150+ vessels damaged or on fire to date (reported)
events.source: Wikipedia — 2026 Strait of Hormuz campaign; Lloyd's List

events.title: Persian Gulf Strait Authority stands up
events.summary: Multinational escort-and-transit authority begins organizing convoys through swept corridors. ~80 mines still reported in the central corridor as of mid-July.
events.flows: Partial, escorted resumption; ~70% of normal transits still missing

events.title: Closure re-declared; blockade reinstated
events.summary: Iran declares the strait closed again; the US insists it remains open and reinstates the naval blockade of Iranian-port traffic. Missile attacks hit two tankers on the Omani coastal route.
events.flows: Non-Iranian transits collapse 108 → 25/week; inbound traffic to 8 vessels (~90% down y/y)
events.price: Premiums hold at 1–7.5% of hull; quotes withdrawn for high-risk tonnage
events.source: Lloyd's List Intelligence Hormuz brief, 21 Jul 2026; crisis reporting
